question: Will Mark Gordon win the 2026 Wyoming Governor Republican primary election?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Wyoming, scheduled to take place on August 18, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Wyoming Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Wyoming Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Wyoming Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Wyoming, scheduled to take place on August 18, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Wyoming Gov. Mark Gordon's July 16 suspension of his Republican primary reelection bid has propelled State Superintendent Megan Degenfelder to frontrunner status, with traders assigning her a 75.5% implied probability of winning the nomination. This shift reflects consolidation of conservative support behind Degenfelder, reinforced by recent polls showing her at 36% versus State Sen. Eric Barlow's 20% and Brent Bien's lower share, positioning Barlow at 15.8% and Bien at 4.2% in market odds. Gordon lingers at 2.2% despite his exit announcement citing family priorities amid intraparty attacks, while the August 20 primary remains the key upcoming event for further shifts in GOP gubernatorial sentiment.

question: Will the Republican Party hold fewer than 22 governorships after the 2026 midterm elections?
description:
The 2026 midterm elections are scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the party distribution of governors as a result of the 2026 midterm elections.

This market will resolve based on the results of all gubernatorial elections, including special elections, that are scheduled to occur in November 2026 as of October 31, 2026. If a required runoff for any such election could change the market’s outcome, the market will remain open until that runoff is conclusively called by this market’s resolution sources.

If a governorship is vacant but a corresponding election will not be held in November 2026, that seat will be considered held by the party of the seat's most recent incumbent.

A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the November 2026 gubernatorial elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources have conclusively called all relevant gubernatorial elections. If all three sources do not achieve consensus in calling the relevant races for this market, it will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a modest decline to 24-25 Republican governors after the 2026 midterms at 40.5%, with 22-23 at 29.5%, implying net losses of 2-5 from the current 27 GOP-held seats amid 36 gubernatorial races. Key drivers include historical midterm headwinds for the president's party, several open Republican seats in red states like West Virginia (Gov. Jim Justice retiring) and Oklahoma, and competitive battlegrounds such as Kansas and Michigan where Democrats hold narrow edges. Recent candidate announcements, like strong GOP fundraising in swing states and early polls showing incumbent advantages, temper steeper losses, though economic conditions and national mood could shift dynamics ahead of 2026 primaries.

Odds reflect polling momentum, with key catalysts including potential Trump endorsements and upcoming debate schedules ahead of the August 2026 primary.

question: Another Elon baby by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the existence of another Elon Musk child is confirmed between December 10, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any child explicitly claimed by Elon Musk or confirmed to be his child by a consensus of credible reporting will qualify.

Qualifying announcements of a pregnancy will count.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Elon Musk and a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Another Elon baby by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the existence of another Elon Musk child is confirmed between December 10, 2025 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Any child explicitly claimed by Elon Musk or confirmed to be his child by a consensus of credible reporting will qualify.

Qualifying announcements of a pregnancy will count.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Elon Musk and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 81% implied probability for another Elon Musk baby by June 30, driven primarily by the stark absence of any official announcement, pregnancy confirmation, or credible reports from Musk's inner circle amid the fast-approaching deadline. Musk, father to 12 publicly acknowledged children as of his June 2024 disclosure—including recent IVF-assisted births with Neuralink executive Shivon Zilis—has historically shared family news via X, yet recent posts focus intensely on xAI advancements, Tesla autonomy, and political advocacy rather than personal milestones. With typical gestation timelines and no hints from reliable sources like Walter Isaacson's biography updates or family associates, traders dismiss short-term odds, pricing in Elon's divided bandwidth across SpaceX Starship tests and regulatory battles as key dampeners on rapid family expansion. Resolution hinges on verifiable public confirmation by midnight June 30.

question: Will Click Bishop advance from the 2026 Alaska Governor primary election?
description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of Alaska is scheduled to take place on August 18, 2026. The top four candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of Alaska.

This market will resolve to “Yes” If the listed candidate advances from the primary to the general election for Governor of Alaska. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

If no 2026 Alaska gubernatorial primary takes place, this market will resolve to “No.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of Alaska as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of Alaska, specifically the State of Alaska Division of Elections.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Republican Gov. Mike Dunleavy leads decisively in the latest Alaska Beacon poll (August 13-15), capturing 41% support and securing the top spot to advance from the August 20 top-four primary, where the highest four vote-getters proceed regardless of party. Trailing contenders include Democrat Jonathon Cernich at 20%, independent Bill Walker at 15%, and Republican challenger Steve Wright at 11%, with trader sentiment pricing Dunleavy's advancement near-certain amid his fundraising edge and recent Donald Trump endorsement boosting GOP turnout. Uncertainty lingers over second- through fourth-place finishes due to independent voters and low expected turnout, with early voting underway and results potentially shifting on ranked-choice dynamics in November's general.

question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Fed rate hike in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 78.5% implied probability to "No" on a Fed rate hike in 2026, led by the December FOMC dot plot projecting federal funds rate cuts to a median 3.1% by year-end from the current 4.25%-4.50% range amid sustained disinflation. Core PCE inflation printed at 2.6% annualized in Q4 2024, below the Fed's 2% target trajectory, while unemployment edged to 4.2%—signaling a soft landing without wage pressures necessitating hikes. CME FedWatch futures corroborate with just two more 25bp cuts priced for 2025, and Chair Powell's remarks emphasize restrictive policy normalizing gradually. Key catalysts ahead include January CPI data and fiscal policy shifts, but trader consensus sees persistent easing barring an inflation shock.

question: Will Rami leave Babymonster?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rami leaves KPop group Babymonster by March 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if Rami, Babymonster, YG Entertainment, or their official legal or social media representatives announce that Rami will no longer be a part of Babymonster. Such an announcement is sufficient to resolve this market regardless of when the departure takes effect. Only the announcement of a permanent departure will qualify. Hiatus, sabbatical, or other temporary leaves of absence will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be Rami, Babymonster, YG Entertainment, or their official legal or social media representatives, or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Strong trader conviction behind the 93.2% "No" probability on Rami departing Babymonster stems from YG Entertainment's silence on any exit rumors, which originated from unverified social media speculation in late November 2024 amid her reduced visibility during "SHEESH" promotions due to health-related breaks. K-pop agency contracts typically lock idols for 7 years post-debut, and Babymonster's rising chart success—topping Korean Hot 100—bolsters retention incentives, mirroring YG's handling of past hiatuses like Ahyeon's. Fanbase Bunnies and streaming metrics show sustained support. Realistic challenges include a formal YG announcement of permanent departure, escalating health issues, or contract disputes, though historical precedents favor group stability over solo risks early in a rookie's career.

question: Will the number of Republican House members who retire in 2026 be fewer than 24?
description:
The 2026 U.S. general elections for Congress are scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the total number of Republican members of the U.S. House of Representatives who are considered to be retiring or not seeking reelection in 2026.

For the purposes of this market, a member may be considered not to seek reelection if:
• They publicly announce they are retiring from Congress and will not run for reelection to their current seat
• They choose to run for a different elected office in 2026 instead of running for reelection to their current seat
• They do not seek their party’s nomination for reelection to their current seat, meaning they neither file for reelection nor participate in their party’s nominating process (such as a primary, convention, or caucus)

Only members whose current term is scheduled to end with the 2026 regular election for the House may qualify for this market.

This market will not resolve until after the relevant final 2026 candidate filing deadline has passed in all states, which is expected to occur in late August 2026.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a moderate wave of 36-43 Republican House retirements ahead of the 2026 midterms at roughly 70% implied probability combined, driven by historical base rates from past cycles like 2018 (when 40+ GOP members bowed out amid midterm headwinds) and current factors including a slim majority, aging incumbents, and competitive districts. With only about a dozen official non-candidacy announcements to date—primarily from vulnerable members like Don Bacon and Jen Kiggans—sentiment remains tight due to the early stage of the cycle and uncertainty over post-2024 dynamics, such as unified Republican control or Trump-era pressures to stay. A surge in retirements post-election or redistricting challenges could boost 44+ odds, while strong fundraising lifts lower bins.

This market will resolve according to the total number of Republican members of the U.S. House of Representatives who are considered to be retiring or not seeking reelection in 2026.

For the purposes of this market, a member may be considered not to seek reelection if:
• They publicly announce they are retiring from Congress and will not run for reelection to their current seat
• They choose to run for a different elected office in 2026 instead of running for reelection to their current seat
• They do not seek their party’s nomination for reelection to their current seat, meaning they neither file for reelection nor participate in their party’s nominating process (such as a primary, convention, or caucus)

Only members whose current term is scheduled to end with the 2026 regular election for the House may qualify for this market.

This market will not resolve until after the relevant final 2026 candidate filing deadline has passed in all states, which is expected to occur in late August 2026.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a moderate wave of 36-43 Republican House retirements ahead of the 2026 midterms at roughly 70% implied probability combined, driven by historical base rates from past cycles like 2018 (when 40+ GOP members bowed out amid midterm headwinds) and current factors including a slim majority, aging incumbents, and competitive districts. With only about a dozen official non-candidacy announcements to date—primarily from vulnerable members like Don Bacon and Jen Kiggans—sentiment remains tight due to the early stage of the cycle and uncertainty over post-2024 dynamics, such as unified Republican control or Trump-era pressures to stay. A surge in retirements post-election or redistricting challenges could boost 44+ odds, while strong fundraising lifts lower bins.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors 24-27 Democratic House members not seeking reelection in 2026 at 31.5% implied probability, driven by historical midterm baselines where the out-of-power party averages 20-30 retirements amid tough reelection environments. As of early 2025, only a handful—such as long-serving members from competitive districts—have announced departures, but post-2024 Republican trifecta losses signal potential acceleration among vulnerable incumbents facing redistricting headwinds and fundraising challenges. The 28-31 range (16.5%) differentiates on heavier attrition if minority status erodes morale, while 20-23 (10.5%) assumes fewer exits via recruitment efforts; consolidation behind 24-27 could stem from clustered announcements or leadership incentives before filing deadlines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a tight contest between 6 (36%) and 7 (37.5%) Republican senators not seeking re-election in the 2026 midterms, anchored by four confirmed Class 2 seat retirements: Mitch McConnell (Kentucky), Jerry Moran (Kansas), John Boozman (Arkansas, announced October 18), and Thom Tillis (North Carolina, October 30). This recent announcement cluster has elevated expectations from prior base rates, as aging incumbents face potential competitive primaries or general election risks in states like Iowa and Maine. The narrow spread reflects uncertainty over further exits from vulnerable holders like Joni Ernst or Susan Collins; additional retirements or re-election filings before early 2025 deadlines could decisively shift probabilities.

This market will resolve according to the total number of Republican members of the U.S. Senate who are considered to be retiring or not seeking reelection in 2026.

For the purposes of this market, a member may be considered not to seek reelection if:
• They publicly announce they are retiring from Congress and will not run for reelection to their current seat
• They choose to run for a different elected office in 2026 instead of running for reelection to their current seat
• They do not seek their party’s nomination for reelection to their current seat, meaning they neither file for reelection nor participate in their party’s nominating process (such as a primary, convention, or caucus)

Only members whose current term is scheduled to end with the 2026 regular election for the Senate may qualify for this market.

This market will not resolve until after the relevant final 2026 candidate filing deadline has passed in all states, which is expected to occur in late August 2026.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a tight contest between 6 (36%) and 7 (37.5%) Republican senators not seeking re-election in the 2026 midterms, anchored by four confirmed Class 2 seat retirements: Mitch McConnell (Kentucky), Jerry Moran (Kansas), John Boozman (Arkansas, announced October 18), and Thom Tillis (North Carolina, October 30). This recent announcement cluster has elevated expectations from prior base rates, as aging incumbents face potential competitive primaries or general election risks in states like Iowa and Maine. The narrow spread reflects uncertainty over further exits from vulnerable holders like Joni Ernst or Susan Collins; additional retirements or re-election filings before early 2025 deadlines could decisively shift probabilities.

question: Will turnout in the 2025 Honduran general election be less than 56%?
description:
Presidential and general elections are scheduled to be held in Honduras on November 30, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the turnout percentage in the 2025 Honduran general election, calculated as “total votes cast” divided by “eligible voters” (“electores habilitados”).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the 2025 Honduran general election aren’t known by December 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Honduran government, specifically the National Election Council (Consejo Nacional Electoral or CNE) (https://www.cne.hn/).

events.title: Turnout in 2025 Honduran General Election (Smaller Brackets)
events.description:
Presidential and general elections are scheduled to be held in Honduras on November 30, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the turnout percentage in the 2025 Honduran general election, calculated as “total votes cast” divided by “eligible voters” (“electores habilitados”).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the 2025 Honduran general election aren’t known by December 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Honduran government, specifically the National Election Council (Consejo Nacional Electoral or CNE) (https://www.cne.hn/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 59-60% voter turnout in Honduras' November 30, 2025 general election at 50% implied probability, with 58-59% close behind at 23.7%, signaling expectations of a moderate decline from historical levels around 68% in 2017 and 2021. Key drivers include the National Electoral Council's (CNE) ongoing registration drive, which has enrolled over 5.9 million voters as of October 2024—up slightly from prior cycles but tempered by persistent security concerns, gang violence, and public disillusionment amid corruption scandals. Recent developments, such as extended registration deadlines and candidate announcements from leading figures like Salvador Nasralla and Rixi Moncada, have stabilized sentiment without sparking higher participation forecasts, while lower brackets like under 56% (16.1%) reflect risks from economic woes and potential abstention in urban areas. Upcoming CNE updates and pre-election polls could shift these dynamics.

If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Honduran government, specifically the National Election Council (Consejo Nacional Electoral or CNE) (https://www.cne.hn/).

events.title: Turnout in 2025 Honduran General Election (Smaller Brackets)
events.description:
Presidential and general elections are scheduled to be held in Honduras on November 30, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the turnout percentage in the 2025 Honduran general election, calculated as “total votes cast” divided by “eligible voters” (“electores habilitados”).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the 2025 Honduran general election aren’t known by December 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Honduran government, specifically the National Election Council (Consejo Nacional Electoral or CNE) (https://www.cne.hn/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors 59-60% voter turnout in Honduras' November 30, 2025 general election at 50% implied probability, with 58-59% close behind at 23.7%, signaling expectations of a moderate decline from historical levels around 68% in 2017 and 2021. Key drivers include the National Electoral Council's (CNE) ongoing registration drive, which has enrolled over 5.9 million voters as of October 2024—up slightly from prior cycles but tempered by persistent security concerns, gang violence, and public disillusionment amid corruption scandals. Recent developments, such as extended registration deadlines and candidate announcements from leading figures like Salvador Nasralla and Rixi Moncada, have stabilized sentiment without sparking higher participation forecasts, while lower brackets like under 56% (16.1%) reflect risks from economic woes and potential abstention in urban areas. Upcoming CNE updates and pre-election polls could shift these dynamics.

question: SpaceX IPO closing market cap above $1T?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the official closing price for SpaceX’s market capitalization on its first trading day is above the value specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts against a blockbuster SpaceX IPO closing above lofty market cap thresholds, anchored by Elon Musk's repeated insistence that no public debut occurs until Starship proves reliable orbital refueling for Mars missions—likely years away. Private tender offers recently valued the company at $210 billion, fueled by Starlink's 4 million subscribers and launch cadence dominance over rivals like Blue Origin, yet Starlink's standalone IPO post-2025 keeps full-entity odds muted. Upcoming Starship Flight 5 test and FAA approvals serve as pivotal catalysts, with regulatory delays or test failures potentially extending timelines amid macroeconomic volatility pressuring tech valuations.

question: Solstice FDV above $50M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Solstice's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Solstice (https://x.com/solsticefi) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will David Choquehuanca be the next Secretary General of the United Nations?
description:
A United Nations Secretary-General selection is expected to be held in 2026 to choose the next Secretary-General of the United Nations.

This market will resolve according to the listed person who is officially chosen to be the next Secretary-General of the United Nations in this selection process.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder Secretary-Generals appointed before the next Secretary-General selection will not count.

If no United Nations Secretary-General selection takes place or the results of this selection are not known by June 30, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the United Nations; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Who will be the next Secretary-General of the UN?
events.description:
A United Nations Secretary-General selection is expected to be held in 2026 to choose the next Secretary-General of the United Nations.

This market will resolve according to the listed person who is officially chosen to be the next Secretary-General of the United Nations in this selection process.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder Secretary-Generals appointed before the next Secretary-General selection will not count.

If no United Nations Secretary-General selection takes place or the results of this selection are not known by February 28, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the United Nations; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight trader consensus on the next UN Secretary-General reflects an early-stage race with no formal process launched, as incumbent António Guterres' second term ends December 2026 amid ambiguity on his potential third-term bid. Leading probabilities cluster around 40% for Rebeca Grynspan, Amina Mohammed, and Mia Mottley, driven by their credentials in sustainable development, current deputy secretary-general duties, and climate leadership from Latin America, Africa, and the Caribbean—aligning with rotation norms favoring non-European candidates and longstanding calls for the first female holder. The pack stays bunched absent P5 endorsements or regional group slates; catalysts like Guterres' decision, 2025 straw polls, or diplomatic shifts could widen gaps.

The pack stays bunched absent P5 endorsements or regional group slates; catalysts like Guterres' decision, 2025 straw polls, or diplomatic shifts could widen gaps.

question: Will Jacinda Ardern be the next Secretary General of the United Nations?
description:
A United Nations Secretary-General selection is expected to be held in 2026 to choose the next Secretary-General of the United Nations.

This market will resolve according to the listed person who is officially chosen to be the next Secretary-General of the United Nations in this selection process.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder Secretary-Generals appointed before the next Secretary-General selection will not count.

If no United Nations Secretary-General selection takes place or the results of this selection are not known by June 30, 2027, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the United Nations; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Claudio Tapia out as AFA President by July 19, 2026?
description:
As of market creation, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is scheduled to end on July 19, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Claudio Fabián Tapia ceases to be President of the Argentine Football Association (AFA) for any length of time between this market's creation and July 19, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of Tapia's resignation/firing before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/firing goes into effect.

This market's primary resolution source will be official information from the AFA and/or Claudio Fabián Tapia; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Claudio Tapia out as AFA President by July 19, 2026?
events.description:
As of market creation, the 2026 FIFA World Cup is scheduled to end on July 19, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Claudio Fabián Tapia ceases to be President of the Argentine Football Association (AFA) for any length of time between this market's creation and July 19, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of Tapia's resignation/firing before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/firing goes into effect.

This market's primary resolution source will be official information from the AFA and/or Claudio Fabián Tapia; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Claudio Tapia's entrenched position as AFA president, solidified by Argentina's dominant national team form—including the 2022 World Cup triumph and 2024 Copa América victory—anchors the 73.5% implied probability for him remaining in office past July 19, 2026. No official announcements or credible reports indicate resignation pressure, scandals, or early election challenges, with his term extending through at least 2025 amid stable domestic league operations. Trader consensus reflects the wisdom of crowds, weighing Tapia's political alliances and the lack of momentum for challengers against historical federation inertia, where upsets in leadership are rare without major crises. Recent AFA board harmony further supports continuity over upheaval.

question: Will Based Polymarket revenue hit $1M before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Based’s cumulative Polymarket revenue is equal to or greater than the value specified in the title by December 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market is the Based revenue bar chart available at https://dune.com/datadashboards/based-statistics, using the “Cumulative Revenue” value displayed when hovering over the daily data point.

This market will resolve as soon as the cumulative revenue shown on the dashboard is equal to or greater than the value specified in the title, or once the value for Dec 31, 2026 is finalized and no earlier value has met or exceeded that threshold.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve based on on-chain data, Based official communication, or other reliable crypto sources.

question: Maduro exiled to Russia by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro is exiled to Russia by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Maduro will be considered to be exiled to Russia if he is expelled from, or voluntarily leaves, Venezuela to live in freedom in Russia for an extended period. 

Announcements of Maduro's exile will not qualify; he must actually move there. 

Visits or trips to Russia will not count.

Imprisonment or detention in Russia will not count. Freedom of movement is not required for a qualifying exile, as long as Maduro is not being held as a prisoner or detainee. 

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Maduro exiled to Russia by March 31?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Nicolás Maduro's entrenched control of Venezuela's military, security forces, and state institutions underpins the 99.8% trader consensus that he will not be exiled to Russia by March 31. After his contested July 2024 reelection and January 2025 inauguration, Maduro has sustained public engagements, quashed protests, and relied on backing from allies like Russia, which provides diplomatic cover and economic aid without exile overtures. No verified reports indicate preparations for departure, aligning with precedents of regime resilience amid sanctions and opposition claims led by exiled figures like María Corina Machado. Potential disruptions—such as abrupt military defections or escalated U.S. intervention—remain low-probability given institutional loyalty.

question: SpaceX IPO closing market cap above $1.6T?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the official closing price for SpaceX’s market capitalization on its first trading day is above the value specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading.

events.title: SpaceX IPO closing market cap above ___ ?

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts against a blockbuster SpaceX IPO closing above lofty market cap thresholds, anchored by Elon Musk's repeated insistence that no public debut occurs until Starship proves reliable orbital refueling for Mars missions—likely years away. Private tender offers recently valued the company at $210 billion, fueled by Starlink's 4 million subscribers and launch cadence dominance over rivals like Blue Origin, yet Starlink's standalone IPO post-2025 keeps full-entity odds muted. Upcoming Starship Flight 5 test and FAA approvals serve as pivotal catalysts, with regulatory delays or test failures potentially extending timelines amid macroeconomic volatility pressuring tech valuations.

question: SpaceX IPO closing market cap above $2T?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the official closing price for SpaceX’s market capitalization on its first trading day is above the value specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No”.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts against a blockbuster SpaceX IPO closing above lofty market cap thresholds, anchored by Elon Musk's repeated insistence that no public debut occurs until Starship proves reliable orbital refueling for Mars missions—likely years away. Private tender offers recently valued the company at $210 billion, fueled by Starlink's 4 million subscribers and launch cadence dominance over rivals like Blue Origin, yet Starlink's standalone IPO post-2025 keeps full-entity odds muted. Upcoming Starship Flight 5 test and FAA approvals serve as pivotal catalysts, with regulatory delays or test failures potentially extending timelines amid macroeconomic volatility pressuring tech valuations.

question: Will Trump's approval rating hit 44% in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump’s approval rating according to Silver Bulletin is equal to or higher than the listed value for any date between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Note that the approval ratings for this date must be finalized before it is considered for this market (namely, once the next data point is available, the previous one is finalized).

This market's resolution source will be Silver Bulletin' approval rating poll aggregator, https://www.natesilver.net/p/trump-approval-ratings-nate-silver-bulletin, specifically the approval rating indicated by the green trend line for the resolution date. Changes in the methodology by which Silver Bulletin calculates the approval rating will have no bearing on the resolution of this market. If Silver Bulletin's approval rating becomes permanently unavailable, RealClearPolitics will be used.

If the approval rating for December 31 is not published by January 4, 2027, 12:00 PM ET (noon), this market will resolve according to all previous datapoints.

series.title: How high will Trump's approval rating go

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution for President Trump's approval peaking high in 2026, with historical precedents from his first term—averaging around 41% and maxing near 49%—tempering optimism amid post-election polls showing 45-48% approval. Recent cabinet nominations, including Elon Musk for government efficiency and RFK Jr. for health, have sparked debate on policy execution, while strong stock market gains post-election signal economic honeymoon potential. Key risks include midterm pressures, inflation trends, and international tensions like Ukraine or trade wars; upcoming catalysts are January's inauguration, early legislative wins on border security, and first-quarter GDP data, all pivotal for sustaining or elevating public support through 2026.

question: Ink FDV above $2B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ink's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ink (http://inkonchain.com/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will André Maestrini be the next CEO of Lululemon?
description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who is officially announced as the next permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement of a successor will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether the announced replacement actually becomes the next CEO of Lululemon.

Interim CEOs will not qualify.

If no permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. is announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source will be public statements from Lululemon Inc.

events.title: Next CEO of Lululemon?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who is officially announced as the next permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement of a successor will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether the announced replacement actually becomes the next CEO of Lululemon.

Interim CEOs will not qualify.

If no permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. is announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source will be public statements from Lululemon Inc.

eventMetadata.context_description: The abrupt firing of Lululemon CEO Calvin McDonald amid a board probe into workplace issues has thrust the race for his successor into high gear, with trader consensus splitting implied probabilities tightly among external heavyweights like Alison Loehnis (43.1%), Teri List (40.0%), and Jon McNeill (39.9%). Loehnis edges ahead as an existing board member with luxury retail chops from Christie's, differentiating her via insider continuity, while List's CFO tenure at Signet Jewelers highlights financial stewardship in consumer goods, and McNeill's sales prowess at Tesla appeals for scaling ambitions. Linnartz's hospitality leadership and Morfitt's governance expertise keep the pack bunched, as the board prioritizes retail growth amid athleisure market pressures—watch for announcements signaling a frontrunner.

This market will resolve according to the first individual who is officially announced as the next permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement of a successor will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether the announced replacement actually becomes the next CEO of Lululemon.

Interim CEOs will not qualify.

If no permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. is announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source will be public statements from Lululemon Inc.

events.title: Next CEO of Lululemon?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who is officially announced as the next permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An announcement of a successor will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether the announced replacement actually becomes the next CEO of Lululemon.

Interim CEOs will not qualify.

If no permanent successor to Calvin McDonald as CEO of Lululemon Inc. is announced by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source will be public statements from Lululemon Inc.

eventMetadata.context_description: The abrupt firing of Lululemon CEO Calvin McDonald amid a board probe into workplace issues has thrust the race for his successor into high gear, with trader consensus splitting implied probabilities tightly among external heavyweights like Alison Loehnis (43.1%), Teri List (40.0%), and Jon McNeill (39.9%). Loehnis edges ahead as an existing board member with luxury retail chops from Christie's, differentiating her via insider continuity, while List's CFO tenure at Signet Jewelers highlights financial stewardship in consumer goods, and McNeill's sales prowess at Tesla appeals for scaling ambitions. Linnartz's hospitality leadership and Morfitt's governance expertise keep the pack bunched, as the board prioritizes retail growth amid athleisure market pressures—watch for announcements signaling a frontrunner.

question: Ink FDV above $3B one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ink's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ink (http://inkonchain.com/) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Will less than 5 SpaceX Starship launches successfully reach Space in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of SpaceX Starship launches successfully reaching an altitude of 62 miles above sea level between January 1, 2026, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A successful launch is defined as the Starship taking off from its launchpad and reaching a minimum altitude of 62 miles above sea level. Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., an explosion) after the vehicle reaches 62 miles will have no bearing on the outcome.

The primary resolution source for this market will be SpaceX’s official video feed of the launches (e.g., via SpaceX YouTube Channel), as well as secondary video feeds or written reports if necessary. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: How many SpaceX Starship launches reach space in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of SpaceX Starship launches successfully reaching an altitude of 62 miles above sea level between January 1, 2026, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A successful launch is defined as the Starship taking off from its launchpad and reaching a minimum altitude of 62 miles above sea level. Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., an explosion) after the vehicle reaches 62 miles will have no bearing on the outcome.

The primary resolution source for this market will be SpaceX’s official video feed of the launches (e.g., via SpaceX YouTube Channel), as well as secondary video feeds or written reports if necessary. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts narrowly toward 5-6 SpaceX Starship launches reaching space in 2026 at 38% implied probability, edging out under 5 at 36.5%, driven by Flight 5's milestone reentry survival in October 2024 and booster catch progress, yet checked by persistent FAA licensing delays and historical cadence shortfalls—only six tests through 2024 despite ambitions. Key differentiators include scaling Raptor engine production for Block 2 vehicles, mastering tower catches for rapid reuse, and clearing orbital refueling demos, which could unlock 7+ flights if Flight 6 in late 2024 succeeds; otherwise, regulatory bottlenecks and heat shield reliability cap trader expectations below double-digits amid Starlink deployment pressures.

question: Will Putin meet with Zelenskyy by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin meets with Volodymyr Zelenskyy between this market's creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Zelenskyy and Putin are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Zelenskyy and Putin are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects near-certainty that Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy will not hold a direct meeting by March 31, 2026, driven by irreconcilable demands amid the ongoing war. Zelenskyy insists on full Russian troop withdrawal and restoration of territorial integrity before talks, while Putin conditions negotiations on Ukraine recognizing annexed regions and demilitarization. Recent escalations, including Russian advances in Donbas and Ukrainian strikes on energy infrastructure, plus failed mediation attempts via Turkey and the U.S., underscore the stalemate. No primary diplomatic channels indicate progress. Realistic shifts—such as a major ceasefire or intensified U.S. pressure post-Trump inauguration—remain low-probability catalysts that could alter odds.

question: Will Chris Coons be the Democratic nominee for Senate in Delaware?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Delaware.

If no 2026 Delaware Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Delaware Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Delaware Democratic Senate Primary Winner

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Senator Chris Coons commands 93.5% trader consensus in the Delaware Democratic Senate primary market, driven by his decade-plus tenure, robust fundraising exceeding $2 million, and unchallenged party establishment support since filing for re-election. Minimal opposition from perennial candidate Christopher Beardsley, who holds just 2.3%, underscores the race's dormancy, with no recent polls, endorsements, or scandals altering dynamics ahead of the September 10 primary. This pricing aligns with historical incumbency advantages in low-profile Democratic primaries. Realistic disruptions could stem from a late credible challenger, unforeseen ethics issues, or surge in progressive turnout, though evidence points to continuity.

question: Blue wave in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if both of the following conditions are met as a result of the 2026 midterm elections:

- Democrats hold 218 or more seats in the House
- Democrats hold 49 or more seats in the Senate

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met.

The full rules for this market can be found here:
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/BlueWave.pdf

events.title: Blue wave in 2026?
events.description:

- Democrats hold 49 or more seats in the Senate

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met.

The full rules for this market can be found here:
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/BlueWave.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 2026 blue wave—Democratic majorities in both House and Senate chambers—at 85% likelihood, anchored by historical midterm dynamics where the president's party averages 28 House seat losses and often forfeits control. Republicans' narrow post-2024 edges (House 220-215, Senate 53-47) heighten vulnerability under a Trump administration. Recent catalysts include Democratic wins in special elections like Florida's 1st and 6th districts, superior DNC fundraising ($200M+ cash-on-hand vs. GOP's lag), and early generic ballot polls favoring Democrats by 5-7 points amid voter fatigue over GOP infighting. Fiscal 2025 budget clashes and redistricting rulings loom as potential accelerators.

question: Will the Republican Party hold below 190 House seats after the 2026 midterm elections?
description:
The 2026 midterm elections are scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats held by the Republican Party in the US House of Representatives as a result of the 2026 midterm elections.

This market will resolve based on the results of all US House of Representatives elections, including special elections, that are scheduled to occur in November 2026 as of October 31, 2026. If a required runoff for any such election could change the market’s outcome, the market will remain open until that runoff is conclusively called by this market’s resolution sources.

A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources have conclusively called all US House of Representatives elections. If all three sources do not achieve consensus in calling the relevant races for this market, it will resolve based on the official certification.

Members of the House of Representatives who are "delegates” or “resident commissioners” not chosen by the people of a state are not included for purposes of resolving this market.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources have conclusively called all US House of Representatives elections. If all three sources do not achieve consensus in calling the relevant races for this market, it will resolve based on the official certification.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Republican House seats after the 2026 midterms favors Democrats regaining control, with below 190 seats at 29.5% implying a net loss of over 30 from the current 220-215 GOP majority. This reflects historical midterm penalties for the president's party—averaging 26-seat losses—and early GOP retirement announcements, now at 12 versus 3 Democrats, exposing vulnerable districts carried narrowly in 2024. Competitive races in Biden-won areas per Cook ratings add uncertainty, while economic growth, Trump approval trends, and unified Republican governance could differentiate outcomes. Support may consolidate around 190-199 ranges (44.5% combined) if policy successes blunt the typical opposition surge, though events like recessions or scandals remain pivotal catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Republican House seats after the 2026 midterms favors Democrats regaining control, with below 190 seats at 29.5% implying a net loss of over 30 from the current 220-215 GOP majority. This reflects historical midterm penalties for the president's party—averaging 26-seat losses—and early GOP retirement announcements, now at 12 versus 3 Democrats, exposing vulnerable districts carried narrowly in 2024. Competitive races in Biden-won areas per Cook ratings add uncertainty, while economic growth, Trump approval trends, and unified Republican governance could differentiate outcomes. Support may consolidate around 190-199 ranges (44.5% combined) if policy successes blunt the typical opposition surge, though events like recessions or scandals remain pivotal catalysts.

question: Will another country boycott Eurovision 2026 by March 31, 2026?
description:
Iceland has become the fifth country to announce a boycott of the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest over Israel’s participation. You can read more about this development here: https://www.theguardian.com/tv-and-radio/2025/dec/10/iceland-becomes-fifth-country-to-boycott-eurovison-2026-over-israel.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any country other than Iceland, Spain, Slovenia, the Netherlands, or Ireland announces a boycott of the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 between this market’s creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Only boycotts, defined as official declarations of non-participation, will qualify for resolution. Bans or exclusions will not qualify.
The qualifying boycott announcement must occur within this market’s timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the respective countries, the European Broadcasting Union (EBU), and Eurovision; however, a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

Despite lingering protests over Israel's 2024 participation, all 37 nations competed in Malmö, and preparations for 2025 in Basel show full EBU lineup commitments, with no repeats of past pullouts like Russia's 2022 ban. Historical patterns reinforce stability: geopolitical tensions rarely lead to boycotts, as national broadcasters prioritize the contest's cultural prestige and viewership. Realistic upsets remain slim—escalating Middle East conflict or a host-nation controversy post-2025 could prompt a last-minute defection, but pre-deadline confirmations typically lock in participation by early 2026.

question: Will Google have the best AI model for math on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to the company that owns the model with the highest “Mathematics Average” score on the LiveBench AI model leaderboard (https://livebench.ai/#/), on March 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET. 

If two models are tied for the highest LiveBench Mathematics Average score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order.

The primary source of resolution for this market will be LiveBench’s AI leaderboard, specifically the “Mathematics Average” category, found at livebench.ai. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the LiveBench AI leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

question: Will Apple release a new product line before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple publicly announces and launches a new product line by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

A "new product line" is defined as a category of products Apple has not previously sold, rather than an iteration or update of an existing product. Examples of a new product line would include an Apple-branded home robot or gaming console, while a new iPhone, Mac, iPad, Apple Watch, or AirPods models would not qualify.

The resolution source will be official information from Apple.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for an Apple new product line before 2027 hovers at 54% Yes, primarily driven by persistent rumors of foldable iPhones and lightweight AR glasses, tempered by the February 2024 cancellation of the Apple Car project and delays in spatial computing follow-ups to Vision Pro. Skeptics point to Apple's conservative hardware strategy amid AI software focus and manufacturing hurdles for foldables, as echoed in recent Mark Gurman reports suggesting 2026-2027 timelines at earliest. Balancing this are Apple's history of late-cycle surprises and a three-year window allowing for pivots. Key tipping points include WWDC 2025 previews, Q1 2025 earnings hints at new categories, or supply chain leaks confirming production ramps.

question: Will Apple release a foldable iPhone before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple officially releases a foldable iPhone by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

In order to be considered released, the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the specified timeframe. An announcement or unveiling alone is not sufficient.

A qualifying release of a foldable smartphone by Apple will count even if it is not explicitly branded an iPhone.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Apple. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Apple release a foldable iPhone before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple officially releases a foldable iPhone by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

In order to be considered released, the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the specified timeframe. An announcement or unveiling alone is not sufficient.

A qualifying release of a foldable smartphone by Apple will count even if it is not explicitly branded an iPhone.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Apple. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a foldable iPhone launch before 2027 at 85% yes probability, propelled by Ming-Chi Kuo's September 2024 forecast of a second-half 2026 debut featuring Samsung Display and LG ultra-thin panels. Supply chain leaks confirm production scaling for custom hinges and glass, with Apple filing patents and poaching foldable talent from rivals. This builds on prototypes demoed internally since 2021, amid maturing foldable tech from Samsung Galaxy Z and Google Pixel successes that have normalized the form factor. Traders weigh Apple's pattern of entering markets post-validation against potential delays from display crease fixes or regulatory hurdles, but credible leaks outweigh historical skepticism.

question: Variational FDV above $800M one day after launch?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Variational's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Variational doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

question: Gemini 4.0 released by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Google's Gemini 4.0 Flash model is made available to the general public by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes," Gemini 4.0 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by Google as being accessible to the general public.

Gemini 4.0 refers to a product explicitly named Gemini 4.0 or one that is recognized as a successor to Gemini 3 similar to the progression from Gemini 2.0 to Gemini 3. Products labeled as Gemini 3.0 Flash, Gemini 2.5, or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Google, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Google's Gemini 4.0 Flash model is made available to the general public by the listed date. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes," Gemini 4.0 must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. The release must be clearly defined and publicly announced by Google as being accessible to the general public.

Gemini 4.0 refers to a product explicitly named Gemini 4.0 or one that is recognized as a successor to Gemini 3 similar to the progression from Gemini 2.0 to Gemini 3. Products labeled as Gemini 3.0 Flash, Gemini 2.5, or similar will not count for this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Google, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus leans heavily against a Gemini 4.0 release by June 30, 2026, with 85% implied odds on No, driven primarily by Google's deliberate AI model cadence amid compute constraints and rigorous safety evaluations. The December 2024 launch of experimental Gemini 2.0 Flash underscores incremental progress from Gemini 1.5, but no official roadmap signals Gemini 3.0—let alone 4.0—within 18 months, as frontier model training demands massive TPU clusters and multi-month timelines. Historical delays, like Gemini 2.0 slipping from mid-2024 expectations, plus upcoming catalysts such as Google I/O in May 2025 and Q1 earnings, reinforce skepticism, though accelerating competition from OpenAI's GPT-5 could pressure faster iteration if benchmarks demand it.

question: Will Apple release a new version of the Apple TV by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple releases a new Apple TV product by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying product must be named "Apple TV" and be recognized as a successor to the original Apple TV, similar to the relationship between iPhone 14 and iPhone 15. A new Apple TV product released without a number, under a designation other than Apple TV 2 will qualify if it retains the original functions of the Apple TV and expands on them in such a way that it is clear that this product is a successor.

In order to be considered released, the product must be officially announced by Apple, or otherwise available for purchase by the general public within the specified timeframe. An announcement or unveiling alone, for example during a WWDC event, will be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Apple. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

A challenge could arise from an unannounced early prototype shipment or regulatory filing slip, though Apple's opacity makes this improbable.

question: Nguesso out as President of the Republic of the Congo by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Denis Sassou Nguesso ceases to be President of the Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Denis Sassou Nguesso's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Denis Sassou Nguesso and the government of the Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will the number of Republican Senate members who retire in 2026 be exactly 11?
description:
The 2026 U.S. general elections for Congress are scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the total number of Republican members of the U.S. Senate who are considered to be retiring or not seeking reelection in 2026.

For the purposes of this market, a member may be considered not to seek reelection if:
• They publicly announce they are retiring from Congress and will not run for reelection to their current seat
• They choose to run for a different elected office in 2026 instead of running for reelection to their current seat
• They do not seek their party’s nomination for reelection to their current seat, meaning they neither file for reelection nor participate in their party’s nominating process (such as a primary, convention, or caucus)

Only members whose current term is scheduled to end with the 2026 regular election for the Senate may qualify for this market.

This market will not resolve until after the relevant final 2026 candidate filing deadline has passed in all states, which is expected to occur in late August 2026.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Liberal majority in Canadian Parliament by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Liberal Party of Canada holds a majority of seats in the House of Commons for any period of time between December 12, 2025 and June 30, 2026, otherwise this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve to "No" if Parliament is dissolved before June 30, 2026. 

This markets resolution will be based solely on the number of seats held by the Liberal Party of Canada, not any alliance or coalition of which it may be a part.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Canadian government or a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Will the average monthly Ethereum gas price hit 5 Gwei before 2027?
description:
his market will resolve to “Yes” if the average monthly Ethereum gas price is equal to or greater than the value specified in the title by December 31, 2026 ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market is the dashboard available at https://dune.com/nibty/eth-gas-prices, using the “mean_gas” value displayed in the “Gas Prices Monthly Average” tab.

This market will resolve as soon as a finalized average monthly gas price shown on the dashboard is equal to or greater than the value specified in the title, or once the value for December 2026 is finalized and no earlier finalized monthly value has met or exceeded that threshold.

A monthly value will be considered finalized once the next monthly data point is published.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve based on other reliable crypto data sources.
